evidence: The paper uses RF variable importance to claim LAR is "the most informative biomarker, outperforming both the serum lactate concentration and the lactate-to-bicarbonate ratio." However, this ranking is robustly corroborated via unbiased individual biomarker AUC comparison: "LAR exhibited superior discriminative performance, with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.82, compared to serum lactate (AUC 0.72) and the lactate-to-bicarbonate (LB) ratio (AUC 0.68)." The same ranking is independently confirmed by SHAP analysis: "SHAP analysis was conducted, which revealed that LAR had the highest feature importance, followed by the LB ratio and serum lactate." The AUC comparison is unaffected by impurity bias.
